body_txt: You're in the presence of an aggressive creature and it's posing a threat to you and other people, so what can you do? You could try to fight and kill it, but you're not a skilled fighter or have a weapon on you. You could try scaring it off, but how so? Fire? Your appearance? Maybe that monster is not intimidated by you, especially if you're outnumbered. The only thing you have with you is a musical instrument and/or your vocal skills. Perhaps, there may be a possible solution. You could try to pacify the beast with mankind's most universal art: music. You ever heard the saying the pen is mightier than the sword? Well, in this case, it's musical instruments. Both methods can be used to de-escalate conflicts without the need for violence. Music, whether its magic or mundane , can soothe savage beasts, fictional or real. The original quote this title originates from is, "Music has Charms to soothe a savage Breast," making the trope title an example of a Beam Me Up, Scotty! . The phrase was coined by the Playwright/Poet William Congreve in The Mourning Bride , 1697. note The quote is used contemporarily still, just cf. the intro of the album " Smash ". See also Magic Music , Mind-Control Music , The Power of Rock , and Super-Effective Lullaby . Compare Snake Charmer .
